an7.64:1.1: “These seven things—pleasing to an enemy, bringing about an enemy’s aim—come to a man or woman who is angry.
an7.64:1.3: “There is the case where an enemy wishes of an enemy,
an7.64:1.6: An enemy is not pleased with an enemy’s good looks.
an7.64:1.7: Now, when a person is angry—overcome with anger, oppressed with anger—then even though that he may be well-bathed, well-anointed, dressed in white clothes, his hair & beard neatly trimmed,
an7.64:1.8: he is ugly nevertheless, all because he is overcome with anger.
an7.64:1.9: This is the first thing pleasing to an enemy, bringing about an enemy’s aim, that comes to a man or woman who is angry.
an7.64:2.1: “And further, an enemy wishes of an enemy,
an7.64:2.4: An enemy is not pleased with an enemy’s restful sleep.
an7.64:2.5: Now, when a person is angry—overcome with anger, oppressed with anger—then even though he sleeps on a bed spread with a white blanket, spread with a woolen coverlet, spread with a flower-embroidered bedspread, covered with a rug of deerskins, with a canopy overhead, or on a sofa with red cushions at either end,
an7.64:2.6: he sleeps badly nevertheless, all because he is overcome with anger.
an7.64:2.7: This is the second thing pleasing to an enemy, bringing about an enemy’s aim, that comes to a man or woman who is angry.
an7.64:3.1: “And further, an enemy wishes of an enemy,
an7.64:3.4: An enemy is not pleased with an enemy’s profits.
an7.64:3.5: Now, when a person is angry—overcome with anger, oppressed with anger—then even when he suffers a loss, he thinks, ‘I’ve gained a profit’; and even when he gains a profit, he thinks, ‘I’ve suffered a loss.’
an7.64:3.6: When he has grabbed hold of these ideas that work in mutual opposition (to the truth), they lead to his long-term suffering & loss, all because he is overcome with anger.
an7.64:3.7: This is the third thing pleasing to an enemy, bringing about an enemy’s aim, that comes to a man or woman who is angry.
an7.64:4.1: “And further, an enemy wishes of an enemy,
an7.64:4.2: ‘O, may this person not have any wealth!’
an7.64:4.4: An enemy is not pleased with an enemy’s wealth.
an7.64:4.5: Now, when a person is angry—overcome with anger, oppressed with anger—then whatever his wealth, earned through his efforts & enterprise, amassed through the strength of his arm, and piled up through the sweat of his brow—righteous wealth righteously gained—the king orders it sent to the royal treasury [in payment of fines levied for his behavior] all because he is overcome with anger.
an7.64:4.6: This is the fourth thing pleasing to an enemy, bringing about an enemy’s aim, that comes to a man or woman who is angry.
an7.64:5.1: “And further, an enemy wishes of an enemy,
an7.64:5.2: ‘O, may this person not have any reputation!’
an7.64:5.4: An enemy is not pleased with an enemy’s reputation.
an7.64:5.5: Now, when a person is angry—overcome with anger, oppressed with anger—whatever reputation he has gained from being heedful, it falls away, all because he is overcome with anger.
an7.64:5.6: This is the fifth thing pleasing to an enemy, bringing about an enemy’s aim, that comes to a man or woman who is angry.
an7.64:6.1: “And further, an enemy wishes of an enemy,
an7.64:6.2: ‘O, may this person not have any friends!’
an7.64:6.4: An enemy is not pleased with an enemy’s having friends.
an7.64:6.5: Now, when a person is angry—overcome with anger, oppressed with anger—his friends, companions, & relatives will avoid him from afar, all because he is overcome with anger.
an7.64:6.6: This is the sixth thing pleasing to an enemy, bringing about an enemy’s aim, that comes to a man or woman who is angry.
an7.64:7.1: “And further, an enemy wishes of an enemy,
an7.64:7.2: ‘O, may this person, on the break-up of the body, after death, reappear in a plane of deprivation, a bad destination, a lower realm, hell!’
an7.64:7.4: An enemy is not pleased with an enemy’s going to heaven.
an7.64:7.5: Now, when a person is angry—overcome with anger, oppressed with anger—he engages in misconduct with the body, misconduct with speech, misconduct with the mind.
an7.64:7.6: Having engaged in misconduct with the body, misconduct with speech, misconduct with the mind, then—
an7.64:7.7: on the break-up of the body, after death—he reappears in a plane of deprivation, a bad destination, a lower realm, hell, all because he was overcome with anger.
an7.64:7.8: This is the seventh thing pleasing to an enemy, bringing about an enemy’s aim, that comes to a man or woman who is angry.
an7.64:8.1: “These are the seven things—pleasing to an enemy, bringing about an enemy’s aim—that come to a man or woman who is angry.”
an7.64:9.4: he turns it into a loss,
an7.64:10.1: having done damage with word & deed.
an7.64:12.4: that his danger is born from within.
an7.64:13.1: An angry person doesn’t know his own benefit.
an7.64:13.2: An angry person doesn’t see the Dhamma.
an7.64:13.4: is in a mass of darkness.
an7.64:14.1: He takes pleasure in bad deeds
an7.64:14.3: but later, when his anger is gone,
an7.64:14.4: he suffers as if burned with fire.
an7.64:16.1: he has no shame, no compunction,
an7.64:16.3: For a person overcome with anger,
an7.64:17.1: I’ll list the deeds that bring remorse,
an7.64:17.2: that are far from the teachings.
an7.64:18.1: An angry person kills his father,
an7.64:19.1: It’s because of a mother’s devotion
an7.64:19.4: can kill this giver of life.
an7.64:20.3: yet an angry person, deranged, can kill himself in many ways:
an7.64:22.2: that kill beings and do violence to himself,
an7.64:23.1: This snare of Māra, in the form of anger,
an7.64:23.2: dwelling in the cave of the heart:
